name: Solana
tagline: A high-speed, low-fee blockchain built for apps that need to move fast. Here's how it actually works — no hype, just the fundamentals and the honest risks.
whatIsIt: Solana is a Layer-1 blockchain — a base network, like Bitcoin or Ethereum, that records transactions and runs programs (smart contracts). It launched in March 2020, built by Solana Labs (co-founded by Anatoly Yakovenko and Raj Gokal).

Its whole design goal is speed and low cost. Where older chains process a handful of transactions per second and can charge dollars in fees, Solana targets thousands of transactions per second with fees that are usually a small fraction of a cent.
SOL is the network's native token. You use it to pay transaction fees, and you can stake it (lock it with a validator) to help secure the network and earn rewards.

paragraphs: Most blockchains slow down because every validator has to agree on when each transaction happened. Solana's key trick is Proof of History (PoH): a cryptographic clock that timestamps transactions before they reach consensus, so validators can process them in order without stopping to negotiate the time.
PoH sits on top of a Proof of Stake system, where validators stake SOL to earn the right to produce blocks. Together they give Solana its roughly 400-millisecond block times and high throughput.
bullets: Fast: blocks finalize in well under a second.
Cheap: typical fees are tiny fractions of a cent.
Single global state: one fast chain, not many shards — simpler for apps.

paragraphs: The big current focus is reliability and scale. Firedancer, an independent validator client built by Jump Crypto, is being rolled out to make the network more resilient and remove single-client risk.
Alongside that, the ecosystem is pushing consumer and mobile crypto — the Seeker phone, Solana Pay, and token-extensions for compliant stablecoins and real-world assets.
bullets: Firedancer: a second validator client for resilience and higher throughput.
Mobile: the Seeker phone and Mobile Stack put a wallet in your pocket.
Token extensions: richer, programmable tokens for stablecoins and RWAs.

quickFacts: Proof of Stake + Proof of History
Inflationary with a declining schedule; staking rewards
ecosystem: DeFi & trading: Jupiter (the routing layer), Orca, Raydium, Meteora — swap and provide liquidity.
Launchpads & memecoins: pump.fun, Bags — where new tokens are minted and traded.
Payments: Solana Pay and stablecoins (USDC) settle in under a second.
Mobile: the Saga and Seeker phones put a self-custody wallet in your pocket.

history.event: DeFi and NFT boom; SOL reaches an all-time high, but the network also hits congestion and outages.
FTX/Alameda — prominent early backers — collapse, hammering SOL's price.
Network reliability improves; memecoin and consumer activity surge back.
Firedancer rollout and mobile/consumer push continue to harden and grow the network.

risks: Outage history: Solana suffered several network halts in 2021-2022 when overloaded. Reliability has improved a lot since, and Firedancer aims to harden it further — but the track record is real.
Validator cost & concentration: running a validator takes serious hardware, which can concentrate who secures the network.
Early-backer baggage: FTX/Alameda were prominent early investors; their November 2022 collapse hammered SOL's price before it recovered.
Volatility & inflation: SOL's price swings hard, and new SOL is issued over time. Never invest money you can't afford to lose.
howToInvest: Get a wallet: Phantom or Solflare. Write your seed phrase on paper — never type it into a website.
Get some SOL: from a reputable exchange, or swap into it. You'll need a little to pay fees.
Start tiny: send a small amount first to learn the flow before moving anything meaningful.
Verify everything: scams are everywhere. Learn to read a transaction before you sign it.
